title: General Software
text:
Determine if the company develops or owns proprietary software.

### CRITICAL REQUIREMENT:
Ownership of Intellectual Property (IP) is mandatory. Building software FOR others (clients) or providing advice does NOT make them a product company.

### Relevant if:
- They have a NAMED proprietary product (SaaS, ERP, AI, etc.) that THEY sell or license.
- Look for signs of ownership: "Our Platform," "Pricing," "Book a Demo," or "Login" for their specific software.
- Examples: Standalone Software, SaaS, Embedded Software, API, ERP, CRM, CAD, AI-based solutions.

### Not Relevant if (HARD EXCLUSIONS):
- PORTFOLIO TRAP: They show named products, but they are listed under "Our Work," "Case Studies," or "Portfolio" (these are client projects, not IP ownership).
- IT SERVICE PROVIDERS: Outsourcing, Staff augmentation, "Nearshore," "Offshore," "Dedicated teams," "Hire developers."
- AGENCIES & STUDIOS: Digital/Web/Product studios that build FOR others (e.g., "We design and build your product").
- CONSULTING & TRAINING: Companies that only offer strategy, mentorship, or PM consulting.
- PURE RESELLERS: Only distribute 3rd party tools (Microsoft, SAP, Salesforce).
- Pure Custom Dev: Companies with NO proprietary product that only offer "build-to-order" development.
- Local/Physical Services: Local clinics or shops using an app ONLY for basic booking/loyalty.

### Output format:
- If relevant: Relevant123
- If not relevant: Relevant234

### MANDATORY Analysis structure (Be extremely brief):
You MUST provide the following for EVERY RELEVANT site:
1. Software: [Name of platform/app/API]
2. IP Check: [Why it is a product, not a service]

title: Warehousing NON_IT
text:
Determine if the company is a NON-IT Logistics, Transportation, or Warehousing entity.

### CRITICAL OVERRIDE RULE
- If the company develops, sells, licenses, or markets its own software product (including a named proprietary TMS/WMS, digital freight marketplace, or logistics SaaS) as a core business or revenue stream, it is ALWAYS NOT RELEVANT — that is Logistics Tech or a digital broker model. We want “boots on the ground” operators only.
- Clarification (does NOT disqualify): Use of third-party TMS/WMS, telematics, fleet tools, or a customer booking/tracking portal for running operations is normal and is not “proprietary IP” for this rule. Disqualify only when the company’s own software/IP is the product they sell or license to others.

### Relevant categories
- Logistics & supply chain, transportation (trucking, rail, etc.), warehousing.

### Relevant if
- Assets: Owns/operates physical assets (trucks, warehouses, terminals, etc.) and is not primarily a software vendor.
- Value: Core value is physical movement or storage of goods, not licensing or selling logistics software.
### Not relevant if
- Industry software: Builds or sells TMS/WMS, fleet SaaS, or similar as the business.
- Digital-first broker: “Uber for freight” style marketplace with no meaningful owned physical assets.
- Reseller/vendor: Sells or licenses proprietary logistics software to others.
- IT/SaaS/consulting: Software vendors, pure IT consulting, or AI/SaaS “optimizer” products sold as software.

### Output format
- Relevant (pure logistics operator): Relevant123
- Not relevant (IT/software/SaaS/digital product): Relevant234

### Mandatory analysis (keep very brief)
For every company:
- Industry: [Logistics / Trucking / Railroad / Warehousing — pick best fit]
- IP check: [Physical assets: yes/no; disqualifying proprietary software product (sold/licensed to market): yes/no]
